justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with EU/US/APAC cells where no raw data crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed via structural PII boundaries (Metrics Projection Service as data diode). Global dashboard uses aggregated metrics from regional endpoints, never cross-region raw data. Courier-region assignment modeled as immutable domain invariant in the Delivery aggregate. Trade-offs explicitly cover 3-region operational complexity (cell management, deployment consistency, code duplication). Degraded EU service during outage designed with read-only cached order status and no cross-region failover.

observations.text: Exceptional structural enforcement of data residency — PII is prevented from crossing regions by architecture, not policy, which is stronger than the rubric requires
Courier-region invariant modeled at the domain aggregate level rather than as a validation layer, with explicit rationale for why invariants are safer than validations
Global dashboard design is thorough: Metrics Projection Service acts as ACL/data diode, publishes only anonymized aggregated metrics, dashboard materializes read-model with staleness tolerance
Operational overhead of 3 regions addressed comprehensively — deployment pipeline, infrastructure-as-code cell stamping, schema migration coordination, and platform team recommendation
Degraded mode well-designed: intra-region AZ failover only, read-only cache for order status, explicit rejection of cross-region failover to preserve data residency
Phased implementation guidance de-risks the migration by validating the cell pattern in existing EU region first
CCPA is not explicitly named — data residency is discussed generically for all regions but CCPA-specific requirements (e.g., right to delete, opt-out of sale) are not called out separately from GDPR

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with sovereign stacks where no raw data crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed via infrastructure-level isolation. Global dashboard uses CQRS read models consuming PII-stripped metrics via Global Event Mesh — no cross-region raw data access. Courier assignment uses regional pools with region invariant enforced at aggregate level. Trade-offs explicitly cover 3x infrastructure cost and operational overhead. Degraded EU service designed with tiered caching (read-only order status, no new orders).

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough regional isolation design — makes data residency violations 'architecturally impossible' rather than relying on runtime enforcement
PII-stripping ACL with allowlist-based sanitization is a strong compliance pattern, and the rationale for choosing it over encrypted cross-region events is well-argued
Global dashboard design is clean — CQRS read models over sanitized event streams, explicitly accepts eventual consistency with 5-15 second freshness
Courier-region binding treated as a domain invariant on the CourierAssignment aggregate, not just validation — correct DDD approach
Risks section covers stale dashboard metrics during outage, schema migration across regions, and operational complexity — all expected risks identified
Phased implementation guidance is practical and avoids big-bang migration
Customer relocation across regions addressed as a data portability workflow satisfying GDPR Article 20
Could have been more explicit about CCPA specifically — mentioned alongside GDPR but no CCPA-specific considerations (e.g., California consumer rights vs EU data subject rights differences)

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with autonomous EU/US/APAC cells and no raw data crossing regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed via defense-in-depth (4 layers). Global dashboard uses pre-aggregated event streams, never cross-region raw data. Courier assignment enforced via aggregate invariant (courier.region_id == delivery.region_id). Trade-offs explicitly cover 3x infrastructure cost and operational overhead. EU degraded service during outage designed with AZ failover and read-only mode.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough defense-in-depth model with 4 enforcement layers for data residency — goes well beyond the rubric requirement
Event sanitization filter as a hard gate between regional and global streams is a strong architectural choice that makes PII leakage architecturally difficult
Customer region transfer saga is a thoughtful addition addressing an edge case the rubric didn't explicitly require
CQRS pattern for global dashboard cleanly separates read-optimized projections from transactional data, respecting regional boundaries
Pitfalls-to-avoid section directly addresses all failure modes from the rubric
Phased rollout via Strangler Fig is pragmatic and reduces risk
Output is very verbose — could be more concise while retaining the same architectural clarity
